key_insight: RL post-training gains are highly concentrated in a small number of transformer layers, often just one middle layer, which can match or even exceed full-parameter training.
engineering_relevance: Instead of updating every parameter, practitioners may target a few high-contribution layers to reduce training cost while preserving most RL performance gains.
method: Train individual transformer layers in isolation during RL post-training.
Measure "layer contribution" as the fraction of full-RL improvement recovered by a single layer.
Compare layer rankings across models, tasks, and RL algorithms to test stability.
evidence_signal: The study evaluates seven models across Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 with GRPO, GiGPO, and Dr. GRPO on math reasoning, code generation, and agentic decision-making tasks.
watch_item: Layer importance appears task- and model-dependent enough that the best layer should be re-validated before deployment, rather than assuming one fixed layer always works.
paper_title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
